title: Review rejection only removes proposed decisions
context: The CLI described review --reject as deleting a draft, but it looked up no record state before deletion. An executable temporary-store replay showed that rejecting an accepted human-confirmed decision exited 0, printed success, and erased the accepted JSON record.
decision: Resolve the target before mutation and allow review --reject only when status is proposed. Missing, accepted, rejected, or superseded targets return nonzero without reindexing or changing storage. Retirement of accepted history remains a supersession/lifecycle operation, never draft deletion.
consequences: Accepted human history cannot be erased through the review queue CLI or its VS Code delegate.
Normal proposed-draft rejection remains destructive and succeeds exactly as before.
Invalid lifecycle targets fail before any store mutation.
alternatives_rejected: Add a force flag for accepted deletion — accepted history should be retired or superseded, and a force switch would preserve the footgun.
Protect only human_confirmed provenance — accepted status is the authoritative lifecycle boundary even for malformed or legacy provenance.
Silently keep accepted records while returning success — false success would leave callers believing the requested mutation occurred.
